title: Sterculia acerifolia
taxonomy: Sterculia acerifolia is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically in the family Malvaceae. This plant is further classified in the order Malvales, followed by the class Magnoliopsida. The order and class classifications provide more details on the plant's evolutionary relationships. Within the family Malvaceae, Sterculia acerifolia is part of the genus Sterculia. The classification of Sterculia acerifolia reflects its botanical characteristics and evolutionary history.
uses: Sterculia acerifolia has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant is known to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its healing capabilities. In some regions, Sterculia acerifolia is used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation. The plant's leaves and bark are often used in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of fever and cough. Additionally, Sterculia acerifolia is believed to have antimalarial properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
